domains.label: Epistemic
domains.definition: Anxiety about truth, evidence, provenance, expertise, deepfakes, misinformation, source authenticity, personalized information manipulation, and the ability to trust shared information environments under advancing AI capability.

constructs.label: Epistemic trust anxiety
constructs.definition: Anxiety arising from the belief that advancing AI systems may make ordinary evidence, testimony, media records, expert claims, provenance signals, targeted information, or institutional fact-finding harder to trust.

required_evidence: Exploratory factor analysis in the preregistered development pilot
Confirmatory factor analysis in an independent confirmation sample
Ordinal omega and corrected item-total evidence after retention decisions
Graded-response IRT calibration with threshold ordering, discrimination, information, and local-dependence diagnostics
DIF and measurement-invariance review for preregistered demographic, AI-exposure, news-exposure, media-literacy, institutional-trust, information-role, baseline-anxiety, and device groups
External-validity, behavioral criterion-validity, and incremental-validity evidence using preregistered comparator variables
Documented item retention decision leaving at least three retained items before construct scoring can be considered
validation_requirements.scoring_rule: This v0.6 multi-item construct candidate may enter item-level, construct, domain, longitudinal, event-study, cross-domain, or overall ANX-Bench scoring only after preregistered observed validation retains at least three allowed items, a validation dossier passes review, item metadata are updated, and a later citable release explicitly lists official scored epistemic items.

release_rules: The v0.6 registry freezes epistemic_trust_anxiety as a multi-item construct candidate only.
The four allowed epistemic items remain development-only until observed validation passes preregistered psychometric, DIF, invariance, criterion, behavioral, and incremental-validity gates.
At least three epistemic items must be retained before construct scoring can be considered.
Version v0.6.0 does not authorize scored, longitudinal, event-study, cross-domain, clinical, individual-level, or overall ANX claims.
